question_text: Which of the following statements best explains why icebergs float in the ocean, even though ice is made of frozen water?

options.label: A
options.text: Icebergs are less dense than ocean water because ice expands when it freezes, making it less dense than liquid water

options.label: B
options.text: Icebergs float because they are colder than the surrounding ocean water

options.label: C
options.text: Icebergs float because ocean water is denser than fresh water ice

options.label: D
options.text: Icebergs float because they contain air pockets that reduce their overall mass

explanation: When water freezes, it becomes less dense (about 0.92 g/cm³) than liquid water (1.0 g/cm³) because the molecules arrange in a way that takes up more space. Since ice is less dense than even ocean water, it floats. Temperature and air pockets are not the main reasons.
